description_long: This object is a partially burned candle with a cylindrical white wax body and a charred, darkened tip where the wick and remnants of melted wax are visible. The candle appears to have been used, as evidenced by the irregular, melted wax and dark residue at one end. The main shaft of the candle is smooth and tapers slightly toward the top, while the base is uneven and surrounded by a cluster of melted and solidified wax, possibly mixed with debris. The wick is visible at the burnt end, surrounded by a dark, ashy area. The candle does not appear to be set in a holder, and the base is irregular, suggesting it was placed directly on a surface. The overall appearance is that of a utilitarian candle, likely used for lighting or as a simple decoration in an urban environment.
description: A partially burned white candle with a charred tip, visible wick, and irregular, melted wax at the base. The candle is not in a holder and shows signs of use.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the narrow cylindrical body and the irregular, darkened base with melted wax.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated white shaft and the charred, melted wax cluster at the end.
description_view_2: Front view emphasizing the upright candle with a visible wick and dark, ashy residue at the tip.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the smooth white body and the uneven, melted wax at the base.
